items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith expected to be charged with war crimes

children.content: One of Australia's most decorated soldiers, Ben Roberts-Smith, has been arrested and is expected to be charged with five counts of the war crime of murder.

gallery.altText: Ben Roberts-Smith in a suit and tie

thumbnail.altText: Ben Roberts-Smith in a suit and tie

items.title: Trump says Iran 'could be taken out in one night' if Strait of Hormuz is not reopened

image.altText: Tankers anchored in Muscat.

children.content: US President Donald Trump says Iran "could be taken out in one night, and that night might be tomorrow night" in his latest press conference. Follow live.

items.title: Trump threatens to jail journalist who broke story of US rescue mission in Iran

children.content: US President Donald Trump is threatening to jail the journalist who broke the story about the rescue of a downed US airman in Iran, as more details about the daring recovery mission are made public.

image.altText: black wreckage in an open landscape with mountains in the background

items.title: They earn $350K but in this city they're 'left with nothing' each month

children.content: US President Donald Trump and New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani are from polar opposite ends of the political spectrum, but comic Ron Barba voted for them both.

image.altText: Zohran Mamdani

items.title: Trump threatens 'demolition' of Iran and takes another swipe at Australia

children.content: Donald Trump says the "entire country" of Iran will be "taken out" if no deal is reached by Wednesday morning (AEST), ratcheting up his threat to target civilian infrastructure and brushing off concerns it would be a war crime.

image.altText: Trump speaking.

gallery.altText: Donald Trump speaks at a podium.

thumbnail.altText: Donald Trump speaks at a podium.

avatar.alt: Brad Ryan is pictured in front of a news studio background, which shows a bridge in a city.

items.title: As Indians queue for cooking gas, a black market is growing

image.altText: A group of people line up against a wall in Delhi.

children.content: Across Delhi, people are waiting days and sometimes weeks for gas cylinders. Yet, the ABC has found a thriving black market, where the same gas can be bought quickly — for those who can afford it.

items.title: PM brings forward his trip to Singapore to shore up fuel supply

children.content: Anthony Albanese
confirms he will be heading to Singapore for a meeting with his counterpart Lawrence Wong later this week. Follow live.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese stands before a flag.

items.title: ASX surges 2pc after modest gains on Wall St as Iran deadline looms

children.content: The Australian surges out of the gates after a four-day long weekend, as traders appear to look through the ongoing uncertainty in the Middle East. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: Giant screen on the wall of the ASX, with large green numbers, showing the price of shares which have risen.

images.altText: Part of the Orion spacecraft with Moon and a distant Earth in the background
Christina Koch silhouette looks back at Earth from Artemis

articles.title: People have travelled further into space than ever before — see what that journey is like for yourself

children.content: If all goes well for NASA's mission, the astronauts aboard could fly the furthest that humans have ever gone. What should we be looking out for?

image.altText: A 3D wireframe rocket with 3 male and 1 female astronauts pose with looming large moon behind

children.content: The Artemis II astronauts have travelled further from Earth than any humans before. The crew is now on a historic six-hour flyby of the Moon, capturing never-before-seen views of its far side. Follow live.

items.title: Views from the Artemis II mission are absolutely 'unreal', astronaut says

updates.title: 'Absoultely spectacular': Solar eclipse comes to an end
View of the Moon eclipsing the Sun 'unreal', astronaut says
Earthshine and solar corona seen during eclipse

items.title: Man sent home from hospital with '10/10' pain ends up losing chunk of leg

children.content: An Australian Navy candidate says he nearly lost his leg following delays in diagnosis and treatment of his emergency condition by two Perth hospitals.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a man's leg with a large scar running down his shin and a brace hanging down the side as he sits on a bench.

items.title: Gambling reforms bill slammed as 'kneejerk' response to Four Corners

children.content: The Northern Territory government has introduced legislation intended to reform Australia's de facto online gambling regulator, but it's been criticised as a "damage-control exercise".

image.altText: A person holding a mobile phone in their hand, with a sports betting app open on the phone.

items.title: Cyclist clinging to reeds, plucked to safety in remote Snowy River rescue

children.content: The cyclist was clinging to reeds in the middle of the Snowy River when the police air wing plucked them to safety.

image.altText: Black and white image of a cyclist in a river

items.title: Fuel thieves targeting NSW truckers as they sleep

children.content: The owner of a trucking company in Western Sydney says most cases of fuel theft have occurred when his drivers have parked on the side of the road for the night.

image.altText: Hand holding petrol cap with cap inside tank

items.title: Woman's 'suspicious' death investigated after car allegedly hit by kangaroo

children.content: NT Police are investigating the "suspicious death" of a woman on a remote stretch of highway in the early hours of Easter morning.

image.altText: An empty highway on a remote landscape, with red dirt meeting the edge of the bitumen.

items.title: Don't ask me about my love life. I'm busy writing a different story

children.content: I might be on a different path, but I can still love and be loved in ways that don't require me to be a mother or a partner.

gallery.altText: Julie stands in a green dress, smiling.

thumbnail.altText: Julie stands in a green dress, smiling.

items.title: Hidden costs mean many comedians make 'zero profit' from festival runs

children.content: Doing a run of shows at the Melbourne International Comedy Festival is the pinnacle for a lot of Australian comedians, but the festival's tens of thousands of punters are rarely privy to the behind-the-scenes costs.

image.altText: Oliver Hunter

items.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?

children.content: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

image.altText: A toy rabbit sits in a field of tulips

items.title: Tech starts to eat itself as AI reshapes workforce

children.content: The wave of change from artificial intelligence rolling across the corporate landscape is going largely unnoticed as attention understandably shifts to an escalating war.

gallery.altText: A silhouette of a person looking at a phone.

thumbnail.altText: A silhouette of a person looking at a phone.

items.title: Council workers stop collecting rubbish across Melbourne over wage dispute

children.content: Thousands of wheelie bins in several Melbourne council areas were left unemptied on Tuesday morning as workers took industrial action over pay.

image.altText: A sign on a fence saying council workers deserve fair pay.

items.title: 'Large quantity of arrows' fired in suburbs after archery club theft, police say

children.content: Tasmania Police recover bows and arrows, and arrest and charge two youths over an alleged burglary at a Burnie archery club.

image.altText: A man holds a compound bow in a forest setting.

items.title: Savannah Guthrie returns to Today show for first time since mother's disappearance

children.content: Nancy Guthrie, believed to be abducted, has not been seen since February 1, as case leads slow.

news anchors sit smiling at desk, wearing yellow

thumbnail.altText: a man and woman, two news anchors, embrace on a TV set
news anchors sit smiling at desk, wearing yellow

items.title: Israeli settler violence in the West Bank deepens against backdrop of Iran war

children.content: Under the cover of regional conflict, Israelis who have established illegal settlements and outposts in the West Bank become emboldened to take over more land as world attention remains on the war in Iran.

image.altText: Mourners sit outside a hospital morgue in the West Bank.

items.title: How China uses ferries and fishing boats to pressure Taiwan

children.content: China has quietly mobilised thousands of fishing boats twice in recent months to form floating barriers at least 300 kilometres long.

image.altText: A graphic features fishing vessels, a target and maps featuring the flags of China and Taiwan.

items.title: Canberra woman in an aged care 'black hole' sees no way out

children.content: After being stuck in a hospital in Canberra for 19 months, paraplegic 84-year-old Mary Wansey has lost hope of ever finding a place in aged care.

gallery.altText: A woman with grey hair and glasses sits in a wheelchair in a hospital room.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with grey hair and glasses sits in a wheelchair in a hospital room.

items.title: No children faced court on new 'adult crime, adult time' offences in past 11 years

children.content: Twelve new offences are set to be added to the government's adult crime, adult time legislation, including offences that no children have faced court on in the past 11 years.

image.altText: Large billboard of man with politcal slogans

items.title: CFA firefighters 'really angry, frustrated' at Easter break-in

children.content: CFA firefighters are "gutted" after thousands of dollars of equipment and fuel were stolen from Terip Terip station over the Easter long weekend.

image.altText: A woman in her 30s looks to her left. She is wearing a blue singlet with black dust and soot on it.

items.title: Pro-climate son-in-law to Rupert Murdoch backs Farrer independent

children.content: Backers of Farrer independent candidate Michelle Milthorpe are celebrating after securing the support of a prominent pro-climate farmer who is also the son-in-law of media mogul Rupert Murdoch.

gallery.altText: Michelle Milthorpe talks to two people with their backs to the camera in the bistro of a pub.

thumbnail.altText: Michelle Milthorpe talks to two people with their backs to the camera in the bistro of a pub.

items.title: Army reservist allegedly worked for Ukraine military without authorisation

children.content: An Australian Army reservist will face an Adelaide court after he allegedly worked for the Ukraine military as a drone operator without authorisation.

image.altText: A man with hands cuffed behind him being escorted by a police officer walking on a suburban street footpath

items.title: 'Just a human being': Day has sympathy for 'hero' Woods over addiction

children.content: Australian number two Jason Day says Tiger Woods's struggles are a reminder that anyone can struggle with addiction, but admits it was "selfish" for the golf great to get behind the wheel.

image.altText: GOlfers Jason Day and Tiger Woods shake hands at the Masters in 2024.

items.title: Residents worry link between minister and wind farm project could dilute advocacy

children.content: Despite the links being declared, some locals worry politicians with a financial stake in a planned development in Tasmania's north will have little sympathy for their concerns about the project.

image.altText: Wind turbines in a field.

items.title: Farmers push for stronger protections in renewable energy policy

children.content: Farmers in north Queensland want a say in how renewable energy companies negotiate with councils, amid concerns poorly designed agreements could have long-term impacts on agricultural land and rural communities.

image.altText: A young woman with a baby on her hip points from her verandah to a mountain overlooking a field of cane

items.title: A luxury beef giant's cattle died of thirst. Regulators were told later

children.content: The lack of investigation into deaths at the $700 million beef business has been described as "completely unacceptable" by a legal expert who argued industry had captured government regulators.

gallery.altText: A herd of AACo's wagyu heifers at Westholme station in Queensland.

thumbnail.altText: A herd of AACo's wagyu heifers at Westholme station in Queensland.

items.title: Tropical Cyclone Maila likely to cross Far North Queensland

children.content: The Bureau of Meteorology is increasingly confident the system will move towards the Queensland coast later in the week, potentially crossing Cape York Peninsula.

image.altText: A satellite image of a large amount of cloud cover in a circle on a map

items.title: Fears for Ningaloo Reef after ex-cyclone leaves animals strewn along beaches

children.content: As sharks swarm in the aftermath, there are fears ex-tropical Cyclone Narelle may have triggered a climate disaster for the world-famous Ningaloo Reef.

image.altText: A dead dolphin on a beach, with debris washed up behind it.

items.title: Zein speaks broken Arabic — here's how she made peace with that

children.content: For Heywire winner Zein, her family is remembered in the fragments she remembers of Syria.
